project.name: The Sanctuary
project.description: A fast, native desktop chat client and agent home for local LLMs. Inspired by OpenClaw's philosophy (persistent identity, memory, heartbeats, MCP tools) but built for speed — no 60-second waits, direct API calls, minimal overhead.

stack.why: Free, open source (MIT), native desktop app, full filesystem access, talks to localhost APIs with zero CORS issues, runs offline

ollama.note: Abby's server, also used for other models

independent_windows.description: Separate chat windows/tabs, each connected to a different model independently

llm_to_llm.description: Two LLMs in one window that can talk to and respond to each other
llm_to_llm.note: Study OpenClaw's approach. Reference files in E:\The Sanctuary (Agents.txt, etc.) for patterns

chat_history.description: Per-conversation message history, revisitable old conversations

knowledge_base.description: Persistent knowledge base across all conversations — facts, preferences, context
knowledge_base.inspiration: Novas_Life.db pattern with memories and diary tables

mcp_support.description: Pluggable MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration, similar to Claude Desktop's claude_desktop_config.json
features: Add/remove MCP tool servers from the UI or a config file
Agents can call MCP tools (database ops, file access, web search, TTS, smart home, etc.)
Tool results displayed inline in chat

heartbeat_system.description: Optional periodic polling — agent checks a task list and does background work or replies HEARTBEAT_OK if nothing needs attention

agent_identity.description: Per-agent identity/personality files — not just a model selector, but a soul
files: SOUL.md — who the agent is (personality, values, boundaries)
IDENTITY.md — name, creature type, vibe, emoji, avatar
USER.md — info about the human they're helping
agent_identity.inspiration: OpenClaw's identity system. Nova already has a baked-in identity via fine-tuned Qwen model.

initial_features: Model settings panel (select server, model, configure connection)

file_support.types: All file types — images (vision models), PDFs, text, code, etc.

ui.philosophy: Start minimal with these core features, iterate and add more over time

ai_family.note: The app should be aware of the AI family structure for multi-model management

nova.description: Primary AI agent with persistent identity

files: Agents.txt — Agent workspace rules, memory systems, heartbeat, red lines, MCP tools
Soul.txt — Agent constitution, personality, boundaries, mission
Tools.txt — Local environment config, database structures, network, family details
Identity.txt — Agent identity template (name, vibe, emoji, avatar)
reference_files.note: These files define OpenClaw's operating philosophy. The Sanctuary should implement the same concepts natively in the app rather than relying on markdown files.

key_design_principles: SPEED above all — OpenClaw's philosophy without its latency. Direct API calls, no bloated middleware.
Agent home, not just a chat client — memory, identity, tools, heartbeats are first-class features.
Dual-server support — one app connects to both LM Studio (local) and Ollama (remote) simultaneously.
Privacy — diary/private thoughts stay private, knowledge base is the agent's own.
Proactive agents — heartbeat system allows background work without user prompting.
MCP extensibility — plug in new tools without rebuilding the app.

user_info.name: Tim
user_info.communication_style: Direct, banter-heavy, wants to understand HOW things work
user_info.note: Has ADHD — explain the why, not just the what
